_metadata.title: U.S. Reportable Diseases Database — Arizona-First with Federal Cross-Reference
_metadata.scope: Every disease on the ADHS R9-6-202 list, cross-referenced against CDC NNDSS, USDA APHIS NLRAD, AZ Department of Agriculture, and AZ Game and Fish where applicable.

sources.doc: Arizona Administrative Code R9-6-202 — Reportable Diseases List
National Notifiable Diseases Surveillance System (NNDSS) Conditions
National List of Reportable Animal Diseases (NLRAD) / NAHRS Reportable Disease List
Arizona Administrative Code R3-2-402 — Mandatory Disease Reporting by Veterinarians and Veterinary Laboratories
Veterinarian Reporting Requirements — Required & Recommended Reportable Zoonotic Diseases in Arizona

_metadata._caveat: Compiled from public sources; intended for the ONE-HealthRecord MVP. Production deployment must keep this database in sync with the live ADHS / CDC / APHIS lists, which update annually. Consider sourcing via API rather than static file in production.

timeline_legend.immediate: Submit through electronic reporting system or by telephone within 24 hours after a case or suspect case is diagnosed, treated, or detected (ADHS lightning bolt symbol)
timeline_legend.one_working_day: Submit within one working day through electronic reporting system or by telephone after a case or suspect case is diagnosed, treated, or detected (ADHS exclamation mark symbol)
timeline_legend.five_working_days: Submit within five working days after a case or suspect case is diagnosed, treated, or detected (ADHS computer/monitor symbol)
timeline_legend.outbreak_24h: Submit within 24 hours after detecting an outbreak (ADHS circle symbol)
timeline_legend.monitored_30d: Monitored disease — report within 30 days (USDA NLRAD)
timeline_legend.notifiable_24h: Notifiable disease — report within 24 hours (USDA NLRAD)

diseases._note: Reportable as an outbreak signal; individual sporadic cases are not.

animal_reporting._note: Eastern, Western, Venezuelan equine encephalomyelitis is required-reportable for veterinarians (ADA).

diseases.common_name: HIV infection and related disease in an infant

animal_reporting._note: ADA + ADHS dual reporting required for veterinary cases.
AGFD/ADHS recommended for animal cases (especially feline plague — cats can transmit to humans).

diseases.common_name: Rabies in an animal (confirmed or suspect)

diseases._note: Outbreak-only reportable; sporadic cases not reportable.

diseases.common_name: Spotted fever rickettsiosis (e.g., Rocky Mountain spotted fever)

diseases._note: Submit within one working day if pregnant (ADHS R9-6-202).

animal_reporting._note: Bovine TB (M. bovis) only; ADA leads on cattle/dairy surveillance.

diseases.common_name: Tuberculosis latent infection in a child 5 years of age or younger
Viral hemorrhagic fever (Ebola, Marburg, Lassa, etc.)

animal_reporting._note: Not formally reportable for animals, but ADHS encourages cross-species sentinel notification given AZ endemicity.

diseases._note: Although technically not on the AZ R9-6-202 explicit list, valley fever is reportable per separate provider obligation under R9-6-201; included for comprehensive AZ One Health coverage.
